experience: Make shortlists adaptive (upper-bound marginal gain → size), use CELF with cached singleton (and selective pairwise) HVs, and prune candidates by cheap upper bounds before exact HV. Implement incremental HV updates or fast specialized HV routines, batch/parallel HV evaluations, deterministic tie-breaks, and small bounded local swap budgets. Diversify a few scalarization weights, but avoid huge weight sets unless approximation budgets are explicit.
current_reflection: Make shortlists adaptive (upper-bound marginal gain → size), use CELF with cached singleton (and selective pairwise) HVs, and prune candidates by cheap upper bounds before exact HV. Implement incremental HV updates or fast specialized HV routines, batch/parallel HV evaluations, deterministic tie-breaks, and small bounded local swap budgets. Diversify a few scalarization weights, but avoid huge weight sets unless approximation budgets are explicit.
good_reflections: Prefilter dominated points, approximate HVC with few weight vectors to shortlist candidates, then perform a local search using cached incremental HVC deltas. Minimize Python-side HV calls by batching and avoid nested HV recomputations. Make the algorithm deterministic (seeded) and ensure it always returns exactly k points.
bad_reflections: Design shortlists adaptively (scale with Pareto size and k), use cheap HVC proxies to prioritize, run CELF lazy-greedy with singleton+selective pairwise caches, and limit swaps to high-potential candidates. Add incremental HV updates or parallel HV evaluation, stronger upper bounds to prune, and avoid Python-side membership in hot loops. Keep determinism and measure HV-call budget versus shortlist growth.

Keep Pareto prefilter, rank by cheap proxies, run CELF with cached singleton HVs, cap pairwise caching to ≤40, size shortlist ≈ c·k (adaptive with Pareto size), prune with fast upper bounds, update hv_selected incrementally, and bound swap iterations. Vectorize dominance/score steps and avoid huge weight sets—use few weights for ranking then exact HV on shortlist.

Prefer a k‑proportional deterministic shortlist, CELF lazy greedy with cached singleton (and pairwise when small), tight upper‑bound pruning, and a bounded incremental swap pass. Reuse vectorized proxies and avoid full HV recomputations inside loops. Validate incremental updates (HV is not additive). Tune weight/shortlist sizes to HV‑call budget; measure HV calls and iterate.
comprehensive_reflection:
- Keywords: CELF, cached singleton/pairwise HVC, adaptive shortlist (≈c·k), tight upper bounds, incremental HV updates, batch/parallel HV, deterministic seed, bounded local swaps.
- Advice: Prefilter Pareto, build a k‑proportional deterministic shortlist using cheap proxies and few scalarization weights, run CELF with cached singleton (limited pairwise) HVCs, prune by fast upper bounds, batch HV evaluations and apply incremental updates; finish with a small bounded swap/local search.
- Avoid: Huge weight grids, full per‑iteration HV recomputation, Python‑side hot loops, unbounded swap budgets, nondeterministic tie‑breaking.
- Explanation: Reduces expensive HV calls and variance, keeps runtime predictable, and concentrates exact HV on a small, well‑pruned candidate set.
